I am sorry to see that your work-related hopes did not develop in the way you expected, but this can often happen in the world of work.
Personally, I think you have put a lot of effort into this and this will certainly serve you well in the coming years.

However, rather than recommending a practical decision for you to make, I would ask if you have ever considered seeing a counsellor or psychologist to weigh up how you feel about the situation.

Ask for this to be the standard tally for other guy as well, and maybe if your bold, have your manager pull in your colleague to render and back his own performance. Though, keep in mind it’s not your job to decide or even make the case for someone to get fired, but that shouldn’t stop you from asking for equal standards professional conduct. Raising the bar of excellence, is like the old saying “you know who’s swimming naked when the tide runs out”.
